description: Add ability to reset the hasEverHadPlugins flag in the side panel so users can see the onboarding flow again.

userStories.title: Add reset onboarding option to side panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to be able to see the onboarding flow again after removing all plugins, instead of being permanently stuck on the 'returning user' empty state.
acceptanceCriteria: When the side panel shows the returning user empty state (no plugins, hasEverHadPlugins=true), a small 'Show setup guide' link is visible
Clicking the link resets hasEverHadPlugins to false in chrome.storage.local
After reset, the side panel immediately shows the onboarding state with the setup checklist
The link is styled subtly (dim text, not a prominent button) to avoid cluttering the UI
userStories.notes:
Edit platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ReturningUserEmptyState.tsx. Add a small link at the bottom of the empty state that calls chrome.storage.local.set({ hasEverHadPlugins: false }) and updates the parent component's state.

The parent component (App.tsx) manages the hasEverHadPlugins state. The ReturningUserEmptyState needs a callback prop (e.g., onResetOnboarding) that App.tsx provides. When called, App.tsx sets its local hasEverHadPlugins state to false, which triggers a re-render showing the OnboardingState.

Example link text: 'Show setup guide' or 'View setup checklist' — keep it minimal.
